notes: The mtn-momo adaptor exposes only a single generic request(method, path, body, options) operation, so endpoint coverage is driven by the MTN MoMo Open API docs plus the concrete paths shown in the adaptor's ast.json examples (GET /collection/v1_0/accountholder/msisdn/{msisdn}/basicuserinfo and POST /collection/v2_0/invoice). MTN publishes interactive Swagger UI and a Postman collection on momodeveloper.mtn.com but does not expose a directly downloadable OpenAPI/Swagger JSON without portal auth, so the spec was authored by hand from documentation (origin=generated). 13 operations modeled: sandbox provisioning (create apiuser, get apiuser, create apikey), OAuth token issuance (collection + disbursement), Collection (requesttopay + status, account balance, account holder active validation, basic user info, v2_0 invoice), and Disbursement (transfer + status). Base URL is the sandbox host; production uses per-market hosts (e.g. proxy.momoapi.mtn.com) with the same paths. The 201/202 asynchronous endpoints (apiuser, requesttopay, invoice, transfer) return empty bodies in reality; an AcknowledgeResponse schema is used so the mocker returns JSON. Field names (amount/currency as strings, payer/payee partyIdType/partyId, availableBalance, financialTransactionId, status enum PENDING/SUCCESSFUL/FAILED) follow the MoMo docs.
